title: Parrlay

{{Quote|The subjugation of Parrlay is just the beginning of our invasion.|Nute Gunray, to Darth Sidious|audio=ParrlayBeginning.flac|<ref name="GB" />}}
'''Parrlay''' was a [[City/Legends|city]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] inhabited by the [[Human/Legends|Human]] Parrlayans. It was located south of the cities [[New Centrif]] and [[Vis/Legends|Vis]], all of which contained [[Transmitter/Legends|transmitters]] used for offworld communications. The city hosted a variety of residential buildings, troop centers, and [[Turret/Legends|turrets]], and it was the hometown of the Human [[Teckla Minnau/Legends|Teckla Minnau]]. The main entrance contained a large archway, and in the northwest area outside the entrance, resources were gathered by [[PKN-49 worker droid]]s.

In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], Parrlay was one of the [[Subjugation of Parrlay|first targets]] of the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] in their [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]]. The city was defended by royal [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]], [[Tuskcat Trooper]]s, [[V-19 landspeeder]]s, and [[Champion (vehicle)|champion]] vehicles. The defenders were unable to stop the forces of the [[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|Trade Federation Droid Army]] led by [[OOM-9/Legends|OOM-9]], who ransacked the city and destroyed the transmitter.

==Description==
{{Quote|We have discovered the Naboo town of Parrlay.|OOM-9|audio=ParrlayDiscovered.flac|<ref name="GB" />}}
Parrlay was a [[City/Legends|city]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]], inhabited by a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Naboo (people)/Legends|Naboo]]<ref name="GB" /> population called the Parrlayans.<ref name="Prima" /> It was some distance from the planet's [[Capital/Legends|capital]], [[Theed/Legends|Theed]], and south of two other cities; [[Vis/Legends|Vis]] and [[New Centrif]]. A stone path from the latter city led directly into Parrlay from the north. A sacred statue<ref name="GB" /> built by the mysterious [[Elders (Naboo)/Legends|Elders]] of Naboo<ref name="Inside">''[[Inside the Worlds of Star Wars: Episode I]]''</ref> lay next to a small [[Water/Legends|lake]] hidden in the [[Forest/Legends|forest]] north of Parrlay. Most of the city was surrounded by [[Tree/Legends|trees]], and a river flowed outside of the eastern treeline. Many of Parrlay's buildings were decorated with [[Color/Legends|yellow]] patterns and designs. Stone paths ran through the city, although many of the buildings were built on [[Grass/Legends|grass]] patches.<ref name="GB" />

[[File:Northwest_Parrlay.png|thumb|left|250px|The northwest area of Parrlay, outside its entrance]]
The northwest portion of Parrlay extended outside of its main entrance. At the northernmost point, there were five [[prefab shelter]]s on an elevated stretch of land. South of them was a [[carbon processing center]] used to store [[Carbon/Legends|carbon]] from the nearby trees, as well as a [[food processing center]] to the south that supported two [[Farm/Legends|farms]]. The resources in that area were gathered by three [[PKN-49 worker droid]]s. The main entrance to Parrlay was guarded by two [[Turret/Legends|turrets]] on each side of the stone pathway. The eastern side of the entrance had two prefab shelters, and the entrance itself was marked by a large archway that had an identical design to those found in Theed.<ref name="GB" />

After the entrance, two obelisk monuments were placed on opposite sides of the path, surrounded by [[Flower/Legends|flowers]]. The northeastern side of the city had a line of five residential buildings, a [[Power core/Legends|power core]], and a [[Barracks/Legends|troop center]] built next to the treeline. The center of the city had three residential buildings and several trees, with the westernmost building surrounded by flowers. The western side of the city had a line of five residential buildings and a temple. One of the residential houses had orange stripes evocative of the stylings of New Centrif. The southern area of Parrlay was surrounded by a medium wall, with a gate on the eastern side guarded by an exterior turret. The top corner behind the wall housed a [[Transmitter/Legends|transmitter]] for offworld and planetary communications. The transmitter rested on an elevated piece of land and was surrounded by a light wall. Inside the wall, there was also a troop center, three prefab shelters, a [[command center]], and a power core, which supported the surrounding buildings.<ref name="GB" />

==History==
{{Quote|OOM-9, we are sending another squad to aid in the destruction of Parrlay.|Nute Gunray, during the subjugation of Parrlay|audio=ComingForParrlay.flac|<ref name="GB" />}}
The Human [[Teckla Minnau/Legends|Teckla Minnau]] was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] in Parrlay to a middle-class [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] family.<ref name="Databank">{{DB|character|teckla|Minnau, Teckla}}</ref> In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], Naboo was [[Invasion of Naboo|invaded]] by the [[Trade Federation]]. Parrlay was one of the Federation's first targets because of its transmitter, which could report the initial invasion to Theed or the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] capital, [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]] ordered the [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-Series battle droid]] [[OOM-9/Legends|OOM-9]] to take a small landing force to destroy the transmitter at Parrlay, as well as those in New Centrif and Vis. After razing those cities with the help of forces led by the [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] [[EEK-176]], OOM-9's [[Army/Legends|army]] marched towards Parrlay, encountering and [[Death/Legends|killing]] two separate groups of three Parrlayan [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] on the path. At the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the invasion, Parrlay was defended by nine royal [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]],<ref name="GB" /> six [[Tuskcat Trooper|tuskcat troopers]],<ref name="CC">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns]]''</ref> four [[V-19 landspeeder]]s, and two [[Champion (vehicle)|champions]], a type of [[Repulsorcraft/Legends|repulsorcraft]].<ref name="GB" />

[[File:Subjugation_of_Parrlay.png|thumb|right|300px|The subjugation of Parrlay]]
Gunray sent a [[C-9979 landing craft/Legends|C-9979 landing craft]] to a clearing near Parrlay's entrance, and when OOM-9's [[Droid/Legends|droids]] entered the city, two heavy [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|beetle droids]], two [[Droideka/Legends|droidekas]], two [[Energy pummel/Legends|energy pummels]], and three [[Armored Assault Tank/Legends|Armored Assault Tanks]] were unloaded from the landing craft to aid in the destruction of Parrlay.<ref name="GB" /> The droids fought their way through the city, killed the Naboo soldiers, and destroyed the labor droids they encountered. When they reached the walls surrounding the transmitter, OOM-9 placed his [[Infantry/Legends|infantry]] troops along the wall while the beetle droids [[Fire/Legends|burned]] through it. More Naboo troopers continued to emerge from the troop center inside the walls, which were gunned down by the battle droids. After a portion of the wall was destroyed, the beetle droids passed through and destroyed the transmitter.<ref name="Prima" /> With the city razed, Gunray reported the subjugation of Parrlay to [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] who secretly orchestrated the invasion. The [[Viceroy/Legends|viceroy]] reported that the city's destruction was only the beginning of the invasion.<ref name="GB" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|We must alert Parrlay!|A Naboo trooper, in unused audio|audio=ParrlayAlert.flac|<ref name="GB" />}}
Parrlay was introduced in [[Prima Games]]' [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|strategy guide]] by [[Steven L. Kent]]<ref name="Prima" />—released on [[November 7]], [[2001]]<ref name="PrimaDate">{{Amazon|url=Star-Wars-Galactic-Battlegrounds-Official/dp/0761537503|text=Star Wars Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide}}</ref>—for the [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'',<ref name="GB" /> released on [[November 11]], 2001.<ref name="GBDate">{{GameSpot|url=games/star-wars-galactic-battlegrounds|text=Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds}}</ref> In the game, destroying the transmitter in Parrlay is the final objective in the first level of the Trade Federation campaign, titled "Beachhead."<ref name="GB" /> The strategy guide described the target of the level as destroying [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceports]]<ref name="Prima" /> instead of transmitters.<ref name="GB" /> The guide also instructs the player to avoid getting sidetracked destroying the buildings in Parrlay, a mistake that could cost them victory.<ref name="Prima" />

If the player attempts the level on the easy difficulty, Parrlay is defended by three fewer troopers and two fewer V-19 landspeeders, with the turret closest to the transmitter absent and royal troopers not spawning from the southern troop center. In the moderate difficulty, the turret and the landspeeders appear, and troops spawn from the troop center. An unused audio file in the game features a Naboo trooper exclaiming that Parrlay must be warned of the incoming invaders. In the original version of the game, the mounted troopers ride [[Kaadu/Legends|kaadu]],<ref name="GB" /> which were replaced with [[tusk cat]]s in the [[2002]] expansion [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns|''Clone Campaigns'']].<ref name="CC" />
